content.text: Every Constitutional Body under this Constitution shall submit an annual report of its functioning to the President, and the President shall cause that report to be laid through the Prime Minister before the Federal Parliament.
The matters to be set out in the annual report under clause
shall be as provided for in the Federal law.
, a Constitutional Body may prepare a separate report in relation to the functioning of each State and submit it to the Chief of State. Part-33 Transitional Provisions
